item.title: Start everyday with a win
item.description: When you start everyday with a win, you've already won before your day gets started.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: You Belong in Tech
item.description: Once a year, tech companies from all over come together to hire black engineers. This year the event took place last week in Pittsburgh, PA.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: When Gmail Goes Away
item.description: If you care about your online presence, own your identity.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: The Fifth Season
item.description: I’ve never read a sci-fi book until last weekend. I cracked open The Fifth Season by N.K. Jemisin before going to bed and got 3 hours of rest that night. How I came to learn about the book was through a good friend, who raved enough about it that I decided to borrow the first two books in the series to catch up with him.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: The Moob Diet
item.description: Here's a weird sentence. I ate myself into a pair of man boobs a few years back. This is a post about how I burned them off (sort of).
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Parenting Advice
item.description: Understand how your child learns. Hire mentors as tutors. Learning starts at home.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Hacking Child Care
item.description: Childcare is expensive. Hack it and share a nanny with other parents.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Python and Diversity
item.description: NUMFocus is a non-profit organization that supports open-source projects like NumPy, SciPy and Pandas
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Thirty
item.description: Whatever you do, choose happiness. It's the best way to live.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Hackers as Parents
item.description: I've recently wondered what goes into being a newborn parent. Now I know and it's like the life of a Hacker.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Twenty-nine
item.description: Today I turned twenty-nine. As a new tradition, I'm writing twenty-nine things I've learned in twenty-nine years.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: 2014 Goals
item.description: This is my first time publicly writing about my annual goals, or any goals for that matter. I typically write them in a notebook or my Mac's text editor, then share them with Tamara. This routine has worked okay, but its largely been ineffective and loose.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Introducing Chrrp
item.description: Chrrp is an iOS app that alerts tech founders when something good happens. Using Stripe's API, Chrrp lets you know when a customer signs up or when you make money. It's like a Stripe pager.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Bye Facebook
item.description: A few weeks ago I deactivated my Facebook account. It wasn't planned or on the to-do list, I just felt indifferent about the service.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Do Product Faster
item.description: My Mom thinks my line of work is magic. When I told her four months ago that I was going to be a Hackstar at Techstars Boulder, she assumed I'd be locked in a room making all the Internets. Well Mommy, it's sorta like that.
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Money, Time, Energy
item.description: Last May, I attended Boulder Startup Week in Boulder, Colorado. The gist of the event is to gather a bunch of tech-oriented folks, attend events ranging from hiking to tech talks to startup parties, and attract folks to the area. Let me tell you
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes

item.title: Always Be Testing
item.description: You're a developer and you get handed over shitty code. The only proof that the code works is someone who uses the software and complains to you that it's buggy. What's a developer to do?
item.dc:creator: Royce Haynes
